title: pimpinella isaurica subsp. sumbuliana
intro: Pimpinella isaurica subsp. sumbuliana, also known as Sumbul, is a plant species within the parsley family, native to Turkey. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sumbul are attributed to its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, Sumbul is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the form of essential oils and tinctures.
